title: Aleuritopteris subvillosa
taxonomy: Aleuritopteris subvillosa is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae, which is a family of vascular plants commonly known as the lacy or lady ferns. This species belongs to the genus Aleuritopteris, which is a group of ferns that are native to various parts of Asia and the Pacific. The classification of Aleuritopteris subvillosa is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum and class are both Pteridophyta and Polypodiopsida respectively. At the higher taxonomic levels, it is classified as a member of the division Pteridophyta, and the clade is Streptophyta. This plant's classification is an essential part of understanding its evolutionary relationships and its place in the plant kingdom.

intro: Aleuritopteris subvillosa, also known as Chinese fox tail or sub-hairy fox tail fern, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. Traditionally, Aleuritopteris subvillosa has been used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities. Aleuritopteris subvillosa is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, as well as being added to traditional Chinese patent medicines.
